title: Flow
description: A Flow describes the valid sequence of operations across the lifecycle of a Resource — which states it can move through and in what order.

resource.description: The Resource whose lifecycle this flow describes.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of this lifecycle.
examples: The standard lifecycle of a loan from application through repayment.
The fulfillment lifecycle of an e-commerce order.

steps.description: The primary sequence of operations in this lifecycle, in order.

branches.description: Alternative paths that diverge from the primary sequence.

from.description: The operation after which this branch becomes available.

to.description: The operation this branch leads to.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of when this branch is taken.
examples: Taken when a borrower fails to repay by the due date.
Taken when a customer cancels before fulfillment.

examples.description: The standard lifecycle of a loan from application through repayment.

branches.description: Taken when a borrower fails to repay by the due date.
Taken when an underwriter determines the applicant is ineligible.

examples.description: The fulfillment lifecycle of an e-commerce order.

branches.description: Taken when a customer cancels before fulfillment begins.

examples.description: The review lifecycle of an insurance claim.

branches.description: Taken when the claim does not meet approval criteria.
Taken when the claimant disputes the denial.
